_comment: Baseline of ACCEPTED parity gaps, keyed by feed id. A 'gap' is a MobilityData ERROR/WARNING notice code that our validator does NOT flag on the same feed. The parity test exits non-zero only when it finds a gap NOT listed here for that feed (a regression / newly-surfaced canonical rule). To accept a new gap intentionally, add its code to the feed's array and commit. Regenerate the suggested contents by running `npm run test:validator-parity -- --write-baseline`. INFO-severity notices are never gating. Regenerated against MobilityData v8.0.1 on 2026-07-12, when the two GTFS-Flex feeds were added; re-run against v8.0.1 after #61 made flex routes long-name-only, which cleared route_long_name_contains_short_name from BOTH flex feeds (it survives only on the two bundled sample-gtfs-feed fixtures, whose naming isn't ours). Every remaining gap below is a feed-hygiene rule (feed_info recommended fields, stops with no stop_time). The flex feeds have ZERO flex gaps: the canonical validator and our validator agree on all 5 injected flex errors.
